title: Anattā predicative vs existential: construction census of Wynne's distributional leg

hypotheses.h1: HOUSE THESIS (Wynne distributional leg, register form). The not-self teaching is carried in the early canon (stratum 1) overwhelmingly by the PREDICATIVE construction — the threefold 'n'etaṃ mama / n'eso'hamasmi / na meso attā' applied to the khandhas plus khandha-applied 'X anattā' — and only marginally by EXISTENTIAL denial ('natthi attā'). As one moves toward the systematizing layers (the maxim 'sabbe dhammā anattā', Abhidhamma stratum 3, and especially commentary/tīkā strata 5-6), the relative weight of generalizing/existential framing rises. Operationally: R = (existential-denial density + maxim density) / predicative density, measured per-Mchar within each layer, is LOW in CANON-mula and RISES across the commentary→tīkā gradient. The commentary read-back diagnostic (atthakathā/tīkā gloss density anchored on the early predicative loci) is POSITIVE: later layers spend disproportionate text re-explaining and generalizing the early supple predicative formula. This is a claim about REGISTER and CONSTRUCTIONAL DENSITY in our held corpus, not about chronology (stratum is a work_slug lookup, T1) and NOT a doctrinal verdict on whether the canon teaches no-self ontologically.
hypotheses.h0: PRE-COMMITTED NULL (reported if it wins). R does not rise monotonically across the canon→commentary→tīkā gradient. Either (a) the predicative construction dominates uniformly at similar per-Mchar density in ALL layers (the formula is quoted verbatim downstream, so commentary just re-cites it — no constructional shift), or (b) existential 'natthi attā' is already negligible-to-absent in every layer including commentary (the existential framing is a modern interpretive overlay, not a corpus construction at any depth), or (c) any apparent R gradient collapses to the generic length/register artifact flagged by the negative controls (it tracks 'sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā/dukkhā' equally — i.e. it is the maxim TEMPLATE moving, not anything specific to anattā or to existential denial). If H0 holds, the finding is: Wynne's predicative-vs-existential distinction is not differentially expressed as constructional density across our layers, and the secondary translator handle is null as predicted.

frozen_lexica.name: P_PRED_triad
frozen_lexica.role: PRIMARY numerator: the predicative not-self triad (this-is-not-mine / this-I-am-not / this-is-not-my-self), all three members, with sandhi + apostrophe + SC/CST anusvāra + macron variants. Counts a passage as a triad hit only via the co-occurrence pass (≥2 of 3 members); the per-member regex below is the union used for density of the construction-family.
frozen_lexica.notes: Member 1 'n'etaṃ mama': covers n'etaṃ / netaṃ / nētaṃ and both anusvāra glyphs ṃ(CST)/ṁ(SC). Member 2 'n'eso'hamasmi': covers spaced/contracted/apostrophe forms incl long final -ī. Member 3 'na me so attā': spaced and fused. The positive (eta-mama-eso-hamasmi-eso-me-attā) clinging formula is INTENTIONALLY excluded here — it is the grasping view, not the not-self predication; if needed it is a separate negative collocation control.

frozen_lexica.name: P_PRED_khandha
frozen_lexica.role: PRIMARY numerator (second predicative form): khandha/āyatana-applied bare predication 'X [is] anattā' — rūpaṃ anattā, vedanā anattā, saññā/saṅkhārā/viññāṇaṃ anattā, cakkhuṃ anattā etc. The construction is [khandha-stem in nom] + anattā. Density of 'anattā' is gated by adjacency to a khandha/āyatana term in the collocation pass.
frozen_lexica.notes: Bare stem 'anattā' (and short-final 'anatta' in compounds/sandhi). Word-boundary \m...\M prevents matching inside 'anattamana' (displeased) — that polysemy MUST be killed in the sense-audit. Khandha gate terms (separate adjacency regex in SQL): rūpa|vedanā|saññā|saṅkhār|viññāṇ|cakkhu|sota|ghāna|jivhā|kāy|man[ao]. Anusvāra both glyphs handled where the khandha is nom -aṃ.

frozen_lexica.name: P_MAXIM
frozen_lexica.role: GENERALIZING numerator: the universalizing maxim 'sabbe dhammā anattā' (all dhammas are not-self). Tracked separately from predicative because Wynne's leg treats the maxim as the systematized/abstracted form.
frozen_lexica.notes: The variant 'sabbe saṅkhārā anattā' exists but is rare/contested; it is NOT counted as maxim-anattā here and instead 'sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā|dukkhā' is the NEGATIVE CONTROL template (same syntactic mould, different mark). Macron on dhammā/anattā mandatory; spaced and fused forms covered.

frozen_lexica.name: P_EXIST_deny
frozen_lexica.role: PRIMARY contrast numerator: existential DENIAL of self 'natthi attā' / 'na atthi attā' (there is no self). This is the construction Wynne predicts is marginal in early canon and (per house thesis) gains relative weight later.
frozen_lexica.notes: Covers fused 'natthatta' sandhi, spaced 'na atthi attā', apostrophe 'n'atthi'. Must NOT be conflated with 'natthi' alone (very common existential negator) — the attā/att adjacency is mandatory. The Vacchagotta avyākata locus (SN 44.10) is the canonical anchor where the Buddha DECLINES both atthi/natthi attā — a key falsifier context (its presence inflates BOTH counts in canon).

frozen_lexica.name: P_EXIST_affirm
frozen_lexica.role: CONTRAST/control numerator (tracked separately): existential AFFIRMATION 'atthi attā' (there is a self) — the eternalist position. Used to show the existential AXIS (atthi vs natthi) is a quotation pair, often co-located, distinct from the predicative axis.
frozen_lexica.notes: \M boundary to avoid 'atthatta'-internal noise; 'atthi attā' spaced and the fused 'atthattā' sandhi. Expect heavy co-occurrence with P_EXIST_deny inside avyākata/diṭṭhi passages — that co-occurrence is itself a measured diagnostic that the existential pair is a doctrinal-debate register, not the praxis register of P_PRED.

frozen_lexica.name: NEG_CTRL_reflexive
frozen_lexica.role: NEGATIVE CONTROL (must be sense-coded OUT, not counted as doctrine): reflexive/oblique attā 'attanā' (instr. 'by oneself'), 'attano' (gen./dat. 'one's own'), 'attānaṃ' (acc.) in ordinary reflexive sense. These share the attā stem but carry NO not-self predication; they must NOT track R.
frozen_lexica.notes: DPD reflexive senses (attan- pronoun). Used to prove the R signal is the construction, not generic attā-stem frequency. If R-bearing layers ALSO show elevated reflexive-attā density, the effect is generic register/length, not constructional — falsifier. Anusvāra both glyphs on attānaṃ/attānaṁ.

frozen_lexica.name: NEG_CTRL_maxim_template
frozen_lexica.role: NEGATIVE CONTROL (syntactic mould): the sister maxims 'sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā' / 'sabbe saṅkhārā dukkhā' — identical 'sabbe + X + MARK' template but the impermanence/suffering marks, which Wynne's predicative/existential distinction does NOT touch. If the R gradient is really just the maxim-template proliferating in commentary, these MUST rise in lockstep.
frozen_lexica.notes: saṅkhārā with retroflex ṅ + macron; ASCII-fallback variant 'sankhara' for any de-diacriticked ingest rows. This control isolates 'is it the maxim FORM moving' from 'is it anattā specifically moving'.

enumeration_sql.label: 00_sanity_nonzero_per_lexicon
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- FREEZE GATE: every frozen regex must return >0 somewhere or the diacritics are wrong (silent-zero check). Run FIRST, eyeball, then proceed.
SELECT 'P_PRED_triad' AS lex, count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$n['’ ]?ē?taṃ|n['’ ]?ē?taṁ|n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|nesohamasmī?|neso['’ ]?hamasmi|na[ ]?me[ ]?so[ ]?attā|na[ ]?meso[ ]?att[āa]$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'P_PRED_khandha', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$\manatt[āa]\M$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'P_MAXIM', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?dhamm[āa][ ]?anatt[āa]$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'P_EXIST_deny', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|natthatta$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'P_EXIST_affirm', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$atthi[ ]?att[āa]|atthattā$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'NEG_CTRL_reflexive', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$\mattanā\M|\mattano\M|\mattāna[ṃṁ]\M$$
UNION ALL SELECT 'NEG_CTRL_maxim_template', count(*) FROM passages WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?saṅkhārā[ ]?aniccā|sabbe[ ]?saṅkhārā[ ]?dukkhā|sabbe[ ]?sankhara[ ]?anicca$$;

enumeration_sql.label: 01_char_denominator_by_role
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Confirm/derive per-role Mchar denominators on THIS dedup view (must reconcile to the stated mula 53.543921 / attha 29.488533 / tika 28.357746). Excludes pli-vism from mula numerator semantics but reports it under commentary separately.
SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula'
            WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'commentary_attha'
            WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika'
            ELSE 'other' END AS layer,
       count(*) AS rows, sum(char_length(original))/1000000.0 AS mchar
FROM passages WHERE is_primary GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

enumeration_sql.label: 02_density_per_lexicon_per_layer
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- CORE TABLE: per-layer per-Mchar density of every lexicon. Magnitude is per-character (T2), never raw rows. layer collapses to the 3 denominators + other.
WITH base AS (
  SELECT id, original,
    CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'commentary_attha'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer,
    char_length(original) AS clen
  FROM passages WHERE is_primary),
den AS (SELECT layer, sum(clen)/1000000.0 AS mchar FROM base GROUP BY layer)
SELECT b.layer, d.mchar,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$n['’ ]?ē?ta[ṃṁ]|n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|nesohamasmī?|neso['’ ]?hamasmi|na[ ]?me[ ]?so[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?meso[ ]?att[āa]$$)/d.mchar AS pred_triad_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$\manatt[āa]\M$$)/d.mchar AS anatta_bare_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?dhamm[āa][ ]?anatt[āa]$$)/d.mchar AS maxim_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|natthatta$$)/d.mchar AS exist_deny_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$atthi[ ]?att[āa]|atthattā$$)/d.mchar AS exist_affirm_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$\mattanā\M|\mattano\M|\mattāna[ṃṁ]\M$$)/d.mchar AS reflexive_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?saṅkhārā[ ]?aniccā|sabbe[ ]?saṅkhārā[ ]?dukkhā|sabbe[ ]?sankhara[ ]?anicca$$)/d.mchar AS ctrl_maxim_pmc
FROM base b JOIN den d USING (layer) GROUP BY b.layer, d.mchar ORDER BY b.layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 03_R_ratio_per_layer
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- KEY METRIC R = (exist_deny + maxim) / predicative, per layer, per-Mchar (denominator cancels but kept explicit). Wynne/H1: R rises canon_mula -> commentary -> tika. NULLIF guards zero predicative.
WITH base AS (
  SELECT original,
    CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'commentary_attha'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer
  FROM passages WHERE is_primary)
SELECT layer,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|natthatta$$) AS exist_deny,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?dhamm[āa][ ]?anatt[āa]$$) AS maxim,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$n['’ ]?ē?ta[ṃṁ]|n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|nesohamasmī?|neso['’ ]?hamasmi|na[ ]?meso?[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS predicative,
  (count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|natthatta$$)
   + count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?dhamm[āa][ ]?anatt[āa]$$))::numeric
   / NULLIF(count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$n['’ ]?ē?ta[ṃṁ]|n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|nesohamasmī?|neso['’ ]?hamasmi|na[ ]?meso?[ ]?att[āa]$$),0) AS R
FROM base GROUP BY layer ORDER BY layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 04_stratum_crosstab_for_robustness
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Edition-robust / lookup-aware view: same densities sliced by stratum() (1early..7other). NOT independent chronology (T1) — used only to confirm the role-based gradient is not an artifact of a single odd work_slug.
WITH base AS (SELECT original, stratum(work_slug) AS strat, char_length(original) AS clen FROM passages WHERE is_primary),
den AS (SELECT strat, sum(clen)/1000000.0 AS mchar FROM base GROUP BY strat)
SELECT b.strat, d.mchar,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$n['’ ]?ē?ta[ṃṁ]|n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|nesohamasmī?|neso['’ ]?hamasmi|na[ ]?meso?[ ]?att[āa]$$)/d.mchar AS pred_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|na[ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]|natthatta$$)/d.mchar AS exist_deny_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$sabbe[ ]?dhamm[āa][ ]?anatt[āa]$$)/d.mchar AS maxim_pmc
FROM base b JOIN den d USING (strat) GROUP BY b.strat, d.mchar ORDER BY b.strat;

enumeration_sql.label: 05_existential_pair_cooccurrence
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- COLLOCATION: do atthi attā and natthi attā co-occur in the same passage (the avyākata/diṭṭhi debate register)? High co-occurrence = existential axis is a quotation-pair, distinct from praxis predicative axis.
SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula'
            WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'commentary_attha'
            WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]$$ AND original ~* $$atthi[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS both_pair,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS deny_total,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$atthi[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS affirm_total
FROM passages WHERE is_primary GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

enumeration_sql.label: 06_khandha_gate_for_anatta
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Split bare 'anattā' into (a) khandha/ayatana-predicated (the real P_PRED_khandha construction) vs (b) ungated (incl maxim, compounds, glosses). Per layer per-Mchar. Disambiguates the bare-stem count.
WITH base AS (
  SELECT original,
    CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'canon_mula'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' THEN 'commentary_attha'
         WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'tika' ELSE 'other' END AS layer,
    char_length(original) AS clen FROM passages WHERE is_primary),
den AS (SELECT layer, sum(clen)/1000000.0 AS mchar FROM base GROUP BY layer)
SELECT b.layer, d.mchar,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$\manatt[āa]\M$$ AND original ~* $$rūpa|vedanā|saññā|saṅkhār|viññāṇ|cakkhu|sota|ghāna|jivhā$$)/d.mchar AS anatta_khandha_pmc,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$\manatt[āa]\M$$ AND original !~* $$rūpa|vedanā|saññā|saṅkhār|viññāṇ|cakkhu|sota|ghāna|jivhā$$)/d.mchar AS anatta_ungated_pmc
FROM base b JOIN den d USING (layer) GROUP BY b.layer, d.mchar ORDER BY b.layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 07_polysemy_audit_rows_anatta
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- SENSE-AUDIT SAMPLE (blind κ): pull bare-anattā rows that may be 'anattamana' (displeased) or compounds, not the doctrine. Random 120 for two-coder κ before any count is trusted (the %carita% lesson).
SELECT id, work_slug, work_role,
  substring(original from greatest(1, position('anatt' in lower(original))-40) for 120) AS kwic
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$anatt$$
ORDER BY md5(id::text) LIMIT 120;

enumeration_sql.label: 08_negctrl_reflexive_audit_rows
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- NEGATIVE-CONTROL SENSE-AUDIT: confirm attanā/attano/attānaṃ are reflexive (DPD pronoun) not doctrinal 'self'. Blind-code 80 to set the κ floor and justify exclusion.
SELECT id, work_slug, work_role,
  substring(original from greatest(1, position('attan' in lower(original))-30) for 110) AS kwic
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary AND original ~* $$\mattan[āo]\M|\mattāna[ṃṁ]\M$$
ORDER BY md5(id::text) LIMIT 80;

enumeration_sql.label: 09_commentary_readback_anchored
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- READ-BACK DIAGNOSTIC: among the early predicative anchor suttas (Anattalakkhaṇa SN22.59, Cūḷasaccaka MN35, Vacchagotta SN44.10), measure how much commentary/tika text is parallel-linked back to them (gloss density proxy via the parallel graph, structural only — no foreign text).
SELECT p2.work_role AS comm_role, pp.relation_type, count(*) AS links
FROM passage_parallels pp
JOIN passages p1 ON p1.id = pp.passage_id
JOIN passages p2 ON p2.id = pp.parallel_id
WHERE pp.parallel_lang='pli'
  AND (p1.work_slug ILIKE '%sn22.59%' OR p1.work_slug ILIKE '%mn35%' OR p1.work_slug ILIKE '%sn44.10%' OR p1.original ~* $$anattalakkha$$)
  AND p2.work_role IN ('attha','tika')
GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 10_translator_handle_secondary
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- SECONDARY (predicted near-null): not-self vs no-self translator habit. Count which English handle each translator uses on passages whose original carries our anattā construction. Framed as translator habit, NOT doctrine.
SELECT t.translator,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* $$not[- ]self$$) AS not_self,
  count(*) FILTER (WHERE t.text ~* $$no[- ]self$$) AS no_self,
  count(*) AS anatta_passages
FROM translations t
JOIN passages p ON p.id = t.passage_id
WHERE p.is_primary AND p.original ~* $$\manatt[āa]\M$$
GROUP BY t.translator ORDER BY anatta_passages DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 11_distinct_work_count_edition_caveat
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Distinct-works caveat: SC = fine per-sutta rows, CST = coarse vagga rows. Report distinct work_slug carrying predicative vs existential per edition so 'how many suttas' claims are edition-honest.
SELECT source_edition,
  count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$n['’ ]?eso['’ ]?ham[ ]?asmi|na[ ]?meso?[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS works_predicative,
  count(DISTINCT work_slug) FILTER (WHERE original ~* $$natthi[ ]?att[āa]|n['’ ]?atthi[ ]?att[āa]$$) AS works_exist_deny
FROM passages GROUP BY source_edition ORDER BY source_edition;

density_method: All magnitudes are per-character, never raw rows (T2). For each layer L in {canon_mula = work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism'; commentary_attha = work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism'; tika = work_slug LIKE '%-tika'} compute density(lex,L) = (count of is_primary passages in L whose original matches the frozen regex) / Mchar(L), where Mchar(L)=sum(char_length(original))/1e6 over is_primary rows in L. Denominators are validated against the stated constants (mula 53.543921, attha 29.488533, tika 28.357746) by query 01 before any density is trusted; a mismatch >2% halts the run. The KEY statistic R(L) = (exist_deny_count + maxim_count)/predicative_count within L (per-Mchar factors cancel, so R is a pure construction-mix ratio; query 03 keeps raw counts visible). The Wynne/H1 prediction is monotone rise of R across canon_mula < commentary_attha < tika. Robustness: query 04 re-derives the same densities by stratum() (1early..7other) but this is a LOOKUP not an independent chronology (T1) and is used only to check that no single anomalous work_slug drives the role-based gradient — a stratum/role disagreement is NOT reported as independent evidence. Edition handling: counts run on is_primary=true to dedupe the sc/cst double-ingest; distinct-work claims (query 11) are reported per source_edition because SC rows are fine (per-sutta) and CST rows coarse (per-vagga). Bare 'anattā' is reported BOTH gated by khandha adjacency (the real predicative construction) and ungated (query 06), and NO anattā-derived number is trusted until the polysemy κ pass (query 07) clears 'anattamana'/compound contamination.
negative_controls: NEG_CTRL_reflexive (attanā/attano/attānaṃ): the reflexive/oblique attā pronoun shares the stem but carries no not-self predication. It must NOT show the R gradient. If R-bearing layers (commentary/tika) also show elevated reflexive-attā per-Mchar, the apparent R effect is generic attā-stem register/length, not the predicative-vs-existential construction. Blind sense-audit (query 08) confirms these are DPD reflexive senses before exclusion.

NEG_CTRL_maxim_template (sabbe saṅkhārā aniccā / dukkhā): identical 'sabbe + X + MARK' syntactic mould but the impermanence/suffering marks, which Wynne's predicative/existential distinction does not touch. If the R rise is really just the maxim TEMPLATE proliferating in systematizing layers, this control must rise in lockstep with the anattā maxim; if it does, the finding collapses to 'maxims proliferate in commentary' (generic), not 'anattā framing shifts' (specific).

Ungated-vs-khandha-gated anattā split (query 06): controls for the bare stem being inflated by commentarial gloss/compound usage rather than the live khandha predication; the construction claim rests on the khandha-gated count.

Existential-pair co-occurrence (query 05): atthi attā and natthi attā appearing together flags the avyākata/diṭṭhi debate register; this guards against reading existential-denial density as praxis teaching when it is actually quotation of an eternalist-vs-annihilationist debate the Buddha declines.
falsifiers: R does not rise monotonically canon_mula < commentary_attha < tika (query 03): if R is flat or non-monotone, H1's register-hardening claim is falsified and H0(a) is reported.
NEG_CTRL_maxim_template rises in lockstep with the anattā maxim across layers: the R gradient is the generic maxim-template artifact, not anything specific to anattā/existential framing — H0(c).
NEG_CTRL_reflexive density tracks R across layers: the effect is generic attā-stem register/length, not constructional — H0(c).

P_EXIST_deny is negligible (<~2 per-Mchar) in EVERY layer including tika (query 02): existential 'natthi attā' is not a corpus construction at any depth, so there is no existential leg to rise — H0(b); the existential framing is a modern overlay.
Predicative triad density does not actually dominate existential-denial in canon_mula (query 02/03): the premise of Wynne's distributional leg fails in our held corpus and the whole contrast is moot.

Polysemy κ < 0.6 on the anattā sense-audit (query 07) with heavy 'anattamana'/compound contamination: bare-stem counts are uninterpretable and only the khandha-gated, hand-cleared subset may be used (raw stem counts meaningless — the %carita% lesson).
Any frozen regex returns zero in query 00 where a hit is expected (e.g. P_PRED_triad): a diacritic/anusvāra error silently zeroed the lexicon and the run halts for regex repair before interpretation.
predicted_signal: PREDICTED (H1, to be confirmed/refuted by queries 02-03): canon_mula shows predicative density >> existential-denial density (predicative dominant), with R LOW; commentary_attha higher R; tika highest R — monotone rise. PREDICTED near-null (secondary): the translator not-self/no-self handle (query 10) shows no systematic doctrinal split, only per-translator house style (e.g. Thanissaro 'not-self', others mixed), framed as translator habit. PREDICTED (read-back, query 09): commentary/tika parallel-links concentrate on the early predicative anchors (Anattalakkhaṇa, Cūḷasaccaka), positive gloss density. TO BE MEASURED (no prediction frozen): the absolute per-Mchar level of natthi attā in canon (could be near-zero, supporting Wynne strongly, or modest); the atthi/natthi co-occurrence rate (query 05); whether the maxim 'sabbe dhammā anattā' is itself more a commentary than a canon phenomenon. The negative controls are PREDICTED FLAT (no gradient) — that flatness is required for H1 to survive.
primary_loci: SN 22.59 Anattalakkhaṇa Sutta — the locus classicus of the khandha-applied predicative triad (n'etaṃ mama / n'eso'hamasmi / na meso attā over the five khandhas); primary P_PRED anchor
MN 35 Cūḷasaccaka Sutta — predicative not-self argued dialectically against Saccaka; predicative anchor in MN

SN 44.10 (Ānanda/Vacchagotta avyākata) — the Buddha DECLINES both 'atthi attā' and 'natthi attā'; the critical existential-axis locus and a falsifier context (inflates both existential counts in canon without endorsing either)
Dhammapada 279 — 'sabbe dhammā anattā', the maxim anchor (P_MAXIM)
SN 22.90 Channa / SN 12.15 Kaccānagotta — atthi/natthi as the two extremes the Buddha avoids; context for reading existential-pair co-occurrence as debate register not praxis
Visuddhimagga (pli-vism) anattā treatment — commentary-shelved-as-mula systematization, key for the commentary read-back
scope_limits: Corpus-linguistic only: measures construction DENSITY and register across HELD layers; it cannot adjudicate whether early Buddhism taught no-self ontologically vs as a strategy — that axis is explicitly off-limits.

Stratum/layer is a work_slug LOOKUP, not independent chronology (T1): a rising R across layers is evidence about REGISTER differences between canon/commentary/tika as we hold them, NOT a dated diachronic claim and not independent corroboration of Wynne's relative-chronology argument.
Cannot touch the redactional leg of Wynne's argument (which texts were edited when) — graph and text density only.
Foreign-language parallels are graph-only (parallel_have=false for lzh/san): the read-back diagnostic counts structural links, it cannot compare Chinese/Sanskrit anattā wording.
Edition coarseness: CST vagga-level rows make 'how many distinct suttas' counts edition-dependent; distinct-work claims are reported per edition, not merged.

Negative results (flat R, null translator split) are first-class findings and will be reported as such; a win for H0 does not refute Wynne, only shows his predicative/existential distinction is not differentially expressed as density in our corpus.
iaa_plan: "Two annotators (operator + one agent coder), blind to layer/work_slug and to each other, code two KWIC samples drawn by queries 07 and 08. (A) anattā sense-audit, 120 rows: label each {doctrinal-anattā-predication | maxim | anattamana-displeased | other-compound | uncertain}. (B) reflexive-attā control, 80 rows: label {reflexive/oblique-pronoun | doctrinal-self-noun | uncertain}. Compute Cohen's κ per task. GATE: κ ≥ 0.70 required before any anattā-derived or reflexive-control density is reported; 0.60-0.70 = report with caveat + adjudicate disagreements; <0.60 = regex/sense definitions reworked and re-coded. Disagreements adjudicated by a third pass against DPD/PED sense entries (dictionary_entries). The κ value and the contamination rate (share of non-doctrinal hits) are reported in the writeup and used to discount the raw stem counts."
